justification: All 6 sections present. Deprioritisation described as a reasonable trade-off with explicit 'given the information available at the time' framing. Five contributing factors identified (external contract instability, accepted risk aging, no input canonicalisation, silent reconciliation failure, no automated integrity check) — exceeds the 4 minimum. No blame language toward Bird, Shaq, or Coach K; Bird's flag is never described as 'ignored'. Forward Commitments include a PROCESS-tagged action (item 5: quarterly review of accepted-risk tech debt) addressing assumption-staleness tracking. Learnings are framed as durable system insights, not blame assignments. The phrase 'should have' does not appear anywhere in the document.

observations.text: Excellent contextualisation of the deprioritisation decision — includes the concrete reasons it was reasonable (provider confirmation, sprint deadline, explicit team decision) rather than just noting it happened
Timeline explicitly states 'This was a reasonable schedule trade-off given the information available at the time' and 'This is the correct behaviour given the sprint scope' for Shaq's implementation
Silent-fail reconciliation job surfaced as an independent contributing factor with clear explanation of why WARNINGs were insufficient
Unannounced sub-provider onboarding surfaced as the first contributing factor with appropriate framing about provider confirmations being point-in-time statements
The learning about deprioritised items needing re-evaluation triggers is framed as a system property ('Accepted risk without a review cadence is forgotten risk') rather than blame
Forward Commitments are comprehensive with 7 items spanning PREVENT, DETECT, PROCESS, and MITIGATE tags, including the critical PROCESS item for quarterly tech-debt risk reassessment
Section 6 (Preserving the Learning) adds durable artifacts — ADR, domain rule, operational checklist — that go beyond just fixing the immediate issue
